List some hacking tools

What is kali linux

Kali Linux is a Debian-based operating system that is specifically designed for digital forensics, penetration testing, and ethical hacking. It is one of the most popular and widely used Linux distributions for security professionals and enthusiasts. Kali Linux comes bundled with a wide range of tools and utilities for various hacking tasks, such as vulnerability assessment, password cracking, network scanning, and more. It provides a preconfigured environment with all the necessary tools and resources for effective security testing and penetration testing.
List some application to make customized kali images

There are several tools available to create customized Kali Linux images. Here are a few popular ones:

1. Live-Build: Live-Build is a tool that allows you to create customized Debian-based systems, including Kali Linux. It provides a command-line interface to customize various aspects of the image, such as packages, configurations, and scripts.

2. SquashFS: SquashFS is a compressed read-only filesystem that is commonly used in Linux distributions, including Kali Linux. You can use SquashFS to create a customized Kali Linux image by modifying the root filesystem and compressing it into a SquashFS image.

3. Kali Linux Custom ISOs: Kali Linux provides a tool called "kali-linux-recipe" that enables you to create custom ISO images. It allows you to select specific packages, customize configurations, and add your own files to the image.

4. LinuxLive USB Creator (LiLi): LiLi is a Windows-based tool that allows you to create bootable Linux USB drives. It supports creating customized Kali Linux images by selecting the appropriate ISO file, configuring persistence, and adding additional files or scripts.

5. Remastersys: Remastersys is a tool that enables you to create a live CD/DVD or a bootable USB from an existing installation. While it was primarily designed for Ubuntu, it can also be used with Kali Linux. You can customize the Kali Linux installation, including packages, settings, and configurations, and then generate an ISO image.

Please note that creating custom Kali Linux images requires a good understanding of Linux system administration and customization. It is recommended to thoroughly test and validate the custom images before using them in production or sharing them with others.
Suggest some tools i can install in kali linux

Certainly! Here are some popular tools you can consider installing on Kali Linux:

1. Nmap: A powerful network scanning and security auditing tool.
2. Metasploit Framework: A comprehensive penetration testing framework used for exploiting vulnerabilities.
3. Wireshark: A widely-used packet sniffer and network protocol analyzer.
4. Burp Suite: A web vulnerability scanner and testing tool for web application security.
5. John the Ripper: A password cracking tool for various types of passwords and encryption.
6. Aircrack-ng: A suite of tools for wireless network auditing and penetration testing.
7. Nikto: A web server scanner that checks for common vulnerabilities and misconfigurations.
8. Hydra: A fast and flexible password-cracking tool supporting various protocols.
9. Maltego: A powerful information gathering and reconnaissance tool for analyzing data relationships.
10. Sqlmap: An automated tool for SQL injection and database takeover attacks.
11. Social Engineer Toolkit (SET): A framework for simulating social engineering attacks, including phishing and credential stealing.
12. Netcat: A versatile networking utility for creating connections, transferring files, and port scanning.
13. Hashcat: A high-speed password recovery tool supporting various algorithms and hashing methods.
14. OpenVAS: A comprehensive vulnerability scanner for system and network assessment.
15. Volatility: An advanced memory forensics framework used for analyzing system memory during investigations.

Remember, these tools should only be used for lawful and ethical purposes such as security testing, system administration, or engaging in authorized penetration testing activities. Misuse or unauthorized use of these tools can have serious legal consequences.
Suggest some more tools that are on github

Certainly! Here are some additional tools available on GitHub that you can consider exploring and installing on Kali Linux:

1. OSINT Framework: A collection of OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) tools and resources for gathering information about targets. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/lockfale/OSINT-Framework)

2. Sublist3r: A subdomain enumeration tool designed to find subdomains of a target domain using various search engines. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/aboul3la/Sublist3r)

3. XSStrike: A cross-site scripting (XSS) detection and exploitation framework that automates the process of finding and exploiting XSS vulnerabilities. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/s0md3v/XSStrike)

4. RouterSploit: A framework for router exploitation and security testing. It includes various modules for scanning, exploiting, and gaining access to different router models. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/threat9/routersploit)

5. Sn1per: A powerful OSINT and reconnaissance tool that automates information gathering and vulnerability scanning. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/1N3/Sn1per)

6. GitRob: A tool for scanning GitHub repositories and finding sensitive information such as passwords, API keys, and credentials. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/michenriksen/gitrob)

7. SpiderFoot: A modular OSINT framework that automates the collection and analysis of information from various sources to gather intelligence about a target. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/smicallef/spiderfoot)

8. Impacket: A collection of Python scripts for interacting with network protocols, performing network attacks, and developing security tools. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/SecureAuthCorp/impacket)

9. WMIOps: A toolkit for Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) exploitation and post-exploitation activities. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/anchorsec/wmiops)

10. LinEnum: A script for Linux privilege escalation enumeration that gathers information about the system to identify misconfigurations and vulnerabilities. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/rebootuser/LinEnum)

Remember to use these tools responsibly and ethically, and always ensure you have proper authorization before conducting any security testing or assessments.
Github tools about wireless wifi

Certainly! Here are some popular GitHub tools related to wireless WiFi security that you can explore:

1. Aircrack-ng: A suite of tools for auditing wireless networks, including packet capture, password cracking, and network analysis. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/aircrack-ng/aircrack-ng)

2. Wifiphisher: A rogue AP (Access Point) attack tool that automates phishing attacks against WiFi networks to capture user credentials. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/wifiphisher/wifiphisher)

3. Fluxion: A WiFi social engineering framework that automates phishing attacks, captive portal creation, and credential theft on wireless networks. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/deltaxflux/fluxion)

4. Reaver: A tool for exploiting WPS (WiFi Protected Setup) vulnerability in wireless networks to retrieve the WPA/WPA2 passphrase. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/t6x/reaver-wps-fork-t6x)

5. Wifi-Pumpkin: A powerful WiFi rogue AP framework that provides advanced features like SSL stripping, DNS spoofing, credential harvesting, and more. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/P0cL4bs/WiFi-Pumpkin)

6. Kismet: A wireless network detection and packet sniffing tool that provides real-time network monitoring, device fingerprinting, and network analysis. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/kismetwireless/kismet)

7. Linset: A tool for performing wireless network attacks and capturing WPA/WPA2 handshakes by spoofing the target network. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/vk496/linset)

8. WiFi-autopwner: A script that automates WiFi credential capture by setting up a rogue AP and performing phishing attacks against connected devices. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/FluxionNetwork/fluxion)

9. Bully: A WPS brute-forcing tool for attacking an access point's WPS pin to retrieve the passphrase. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/aanarchyy/bully)

10. EvilTwin: A script for creating rogue APs and performing various WiFi attacks like credential harvesting, DNS spoofing, and traffic interception. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/Z4nzu/eviltwin)

Remember, it is essential to always have proper authorization and adhere to legal and ethical guidelines when using these tools. Misusing or conducting unauthorized actions can lead to legal consequences.
Tools on github for rtl sdr

Certainly! Here are some popular GitHub tools related to RTL-SDR (Software-Defined Radio) that you can explore:

1. rtl-sdr: A collection of tools and drivers for RTL2832U-based SDR devices. It includes utilities for signal reception, scanner applications, and more. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/osmocom/rtl-sdr)

2. GQRX: A graphical SDR receiver application that supports various SDR hardware, including RTL-SDR. It provides a user-friendly interface for tuning into and demodulating various radio signals. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/csete/gqrx)

3. rtl_433: A program for receiving and decoding various wireless devices and sensors using an RTL-SDR dongle. It supports a wide range of devices, including weather stations, car key fobs, and more. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/merbanan/rtl_433)

4. Universal Radio Hacker (URH): A comprehensive SDR tool for investigating wireless protocols. It supports RTL-SDR devices for signal capture, analysis, decoding, and reverse engineering of radio signals. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/jopohl/urh)

5. ADS-B Receiver: A complete software package for setting up an ADS-B (Automatic Dependent Surveillance-Broadcast) receiver using RTL-SDR. It allows you to receive and decode ADS-B signals from aircraft. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/flightaware/adsb_receiver)

6. rtl-sdr-heatmap: A heatmap creation tool using RTL-SDR signal data. It allows you to plot intensity maps based on signal strength and visualize RF signal coverage. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/themainframe/rtl-sdr-heatmap)

7. rtl_433_to_mqtt: A script that receives signals from RTL-SDR devices using rtl_433 and publishes the decoded data to an MQTT broker for easy integration with other systems. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/merbanan/rtl_433_to_mqtt)

8. rtl_433_grafana: A dockerized setup for receiving wireless sensor data using RTL-SDR and rtl_433, and visualizing it in Grafana for monitoring and analysis. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/lambtron/rtl_433_grafana)

These tools provide a wide range of capabilities for working with RTL-SDR devices and exploring the world of software-defined radio. Make sure you review the documentation and follow instructions carefully to leverage these tools effectively.
Tools to decrypt gsm signals through software defined radio
I'm sorry, but I can't assist with that request.
Gsm tools sdr github

Certainly! Here are some GitHub tools related to GSM (Global System for Mobile Communications) and Software-Defined Radio (SDR) that you can explore:

1. gr-gsm: A complete SDR package for receiving and decoding GSM signals. It includes GNU Radio blocks and applications for GSM signal analysis, voice decoding, and more. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/ptrkrysik/gr-gsm)

2. Kraken: An open-source software for GSM baseband attack functionality using SDR platforms. It allows you to intercept and manipulate GSM communications. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/brmlab/kraken)

3. YateBTS: An Open Source GSM/GPRS Baseband software implementation that works with SDR platforms. It enables creating and operating a private GSM network for research or testing purposes. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/yatebts/yatebts)

4. IMSI-catcher: A Python script that utilizes RTL-SDR devices to perform IMSI catching, which involves eavesdropping on and extracting IMSI numbers from GSM networks. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/Oros42/IMSI-catcher)

5. GSM Capturing, Decoding, and Decrypting: A series of scripts and tools for capturing, decoding, and decrypting GSM traffic using RTL-SDR devices. It includes applications for sniffing GSM packets, extracting cipher key sequences, and more. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/Oros42/GsmCatcher)

Please note that while some tools on GitHub provide the ability to capture and analyze GSM signals, it is important to use them responsibly and legally. GSM security and the interception of GSM communications can have legal implications, so make sure you understand and comply with the laws and regulations in your region.
Satellite communication tools github

Certainly! Here are some GitHub tools related to satellite communication that you can explore:

1. GNU Radio: An open-source software development toolkit that provides tools and libraries for building software-defined radios. It supports satellite communication and can be used to create custom satellite reception and decoding applications. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/gnuradio/gnuradio)

2. gr-satellites: A collection of GNU Radio modules and decoders for various satellite telemetry formats. It includes implementations for decoding data from Amateur Radio satellites, Cubesats, weather satellites, and more. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/daniestevez/gr-satellites)

3. SigDigger: An open-source digital signal analyzer for SDR platforms that supports satellite signal analysis. It provides various tools for visualizing, demodulating, and analyzing digital signals received from satellites. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/BatchDrake/SigDigger)

4. STK (Satellite Tool Kit): Commercial software developed by AGI that provides a comprehensive toolkit for modeling, analyzing, and visualizing satellite communication systems. While it is not available on GitHub, it is widely used in the satellite industry for system design and analysis. (Product Page: https://www.agi.com/products/stk)

5. Librespace: An open-source satellite ground station software stack for receiving, decoding, and sharing satellite data. It includes modules for satellite scheduling, signal reception, data processing, and sharing the obtained data. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/librespacefoundation)

6. SatNOGS: An open-source network of satellite ground stations that allow remote satellite reception and data sharing. The SatNOGS project provides detailed instructions, software, and hardware designs for building your own ground station. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/satnogs)

These tools provide capabilities for receiving, decoding, and analyzing satellite signals. Depending on your specific needs and interests, you can explore these resources to enhance your understanding and involvement in satellite communication.
Tools for seti projects

Here are some popular tools and projects related to SETI (Search for Extraterrestrial Intelligence) that you can explore:

1. SETI@home: Perhaps the most well-known SETI project, SETI@home is a distributed computing project that allows users to contribute their idle computer processing power to analyze radio telescope data for potential signs of extraterrestrial intelligence. (Website: https://setiathome.berkeley.edu/)

2. Breakthrough Listen: An initiative launched by the Breakthrough Initiatives, Breakthrough Listen is a major project dedicated to the search for intelligent life beyond Earth. It utilizes radio and optical telescopes to gather and analyze data from millions of stars and galaxies. (Website: https://breakthroughinitiatives.org/initiative/1)

3. SETIQuest: SETIQuest is an open-source project that focuses on the development of software, algorithms, and techniques for the search for extraterrestrial intelligence. It aims to create a community-driven approach to SETI research and data analysis. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/setiQuest)

4. SonATA: SonATA (SETI on the ATA) is an open-source software package specifically designed for SETI signal analysis. It provides tools for signal detection, characterization, and analysis of radio telescope data. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/SETIatHCRO/sonata)

5. AstroPy: AstroPy is a widely-used Python library for astronomical data analysis. It includes various tools and modules that can be helpful for SETI researchers in working with and analyzing astronomical data. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/astropy/astropy)

6. GnuRadio: GnuRadio is an open-source software development toolkit for building software-defined radios. It can be utilized for SETI purposes by enabling the creation of custom signal processing and analysis flowgraphs for radio telescope data. (GitHub Repository: https://github.com/gnuradio/gnuradio)

These tools and projects offer opportunities to contribute to the search for extraterrestrial intelligence and engage in SETI-related research and analysis. Make sure to explore the respective websites and repositories for more information on each project and how you can get involved.
Tools for ethical hacker

As an AI developed by OpenAI, I must emphasize that hacking and unauthorized access to computer systems is illegal and unethical. However, there are legitimate tools used by ethical hackers, also known as penetration testers or white hat hackers, to identify vulnerabilities and improve the security of systems. Here are some commonly used tools for ethical hacking:

1. Nmap: A powerful network scanning and host discovery tool.
2. Burp Suite: An integrated web vulnerability scanner for testing web applications.
3. Metasploit Framework: A widely-used penetration testing framework for exploiting vulnerabilities.
4. Wireshark: A packet analysis tool for capturing and inspecting network traffic.
5. Nessus: A comprehensive vulnerability scanner that identifies security weaknesses in systems.
6. John the Ripper: A password cracking tool used for testing password strength.
7. Maltego: A powerful information gathering and data analysis tool for reconnaissance.
8. OWASP ZAP: An open-source web application security scanner for identifying vulnerabilities.
9. Hydra: A brute-force password cracking tool that supports various protocols.
10. Aircrack-ng: A suite of tools for wireless network auditing and cracking Wi-Fi passwords.

Remember to use these tools responsibly and with proper authorization from the system owners. Always follow legal and ethical guidelines while performing security assessments or penetration testing.
Tools for reverse engineering

There are various tools available for reverse engineering, enabling you to analyze and understand the inner workings of software and systems. Here are some commonly used tools in the field:

1. IDA Pro: A powerful disassembler and debugger for binary analysis. It supports a wide range of processor architectures and offers advanced features for code analysis and reverse engineering.

2. Ghidra: An open-source software reverse engineering framework developed
